target: data/sections.json part8b.html — two targeted substring replacements inside the L3a Fail-clause double-bind paragraph to own authorship of the codified Fail-clause (instead of framing it as a dome concession) and to reroute the disambiguation burden onto PRED-095 as registered (which publishes no Chapman-language Fail criterion). Resolves ISS-1422 (SEC-8b.3 c5 H1 moderate).
patch_instructions: Two edits inside data/sections.json → part8b.html, both inside the L3a magnetic non-discrimination / Fail-clause double-bind paragraph (sub-paragraph after the threshold-codification paragraph). Patch A replaces the opening sentence of the Fail-clause double-bind block. Patch B replaces the closing-cue sentence 'The dome does not publish which reading governs...' Each anchor was verified to occur exactly once in part8b.html. Interpretation-A and interpretation-B sentences between the two anchors are unchanged. The threshold-codification paragraph upstream (which already says 'our Pass band') is unchanged. Other subsections (8b.1, 8b.2, 8b.4) are unchanged.

patches.before: The Fail-clause concession that “signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone” would falsify the claim admits two natural readings, and the dome does not disambiguate between them.
patches.after: Our codified Fail-clause — the phrase “signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone” that we appended to our Pass band — admits two natural readings, and the dome has not published any falsification criterion specific enough to disambiguate (PRED-095 as registered in data/predictions.json contains no Chapman-language Fail criterion at all, so any fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below).
patches.rationale: c5 Hole 1 moderate recommendation verbatim (trimmed to the operative sentence). Old sentence called the Fail-clause a 'concession' that 'the dome does not disambiguate' — both clauses implicitly attribute the clause to the dome author. But the clause is OUR codification (the preceding threshold-codification paragraph explicitly says 'our Pass band' with the Fail-clause embedded in the quoted 'our' definition). New sentence owns the codification ('Our codified Fail-clause ... the phrase ... that we appended') and reroutes the disambiguation burden via a parenthetical noting that PRED-095 as published contains no Chapman-language Fail language. The forced-disjunction / double-bind conclusion that follows is unchanged, so the section's structural argument is preserved while the rhetorical anchoring is corrected.

patches.before: The dome does not publish which reading governs, and under either reading the Pass band fails to distinguish dome from globe — reading A surrenders discrimination to preserve consistency; reading B attempts discrimination by making Pass and Fail incompatible.
patches.after: The dome has not committed to either disambiguation, and under either reading the Pass band fails to distinguish dome from globe — reading A surrenders discrimination to preserve consistency; reading B attempts discrimination by making Pass and Fail incompatible.
patches.rationale: c5 Hole 1 second-pass edit. Old sentence said 'The dome does not publish which reading governs' — same attribution misframing as the opening (dome being faulted for not disambiguating our codification). New sentence says 'The dome has not committed to either disambiguation' which (a) does not imply the dome authored the underlying codification, (b) still captures the operative point that the dome has not narrowed the falsifier to rule out either reading, (c) preserves the forced-disjunction conclusion verbatim. The interpretation-A-surrenders-discrimination / interpretation-B-makes-Pass-and-Fail-incompatible clauses that follow are unchanged.

holes_addressed.hole: ISS-1422 / SEC-8b.3 c5 Hole 1 (moderate) — Fail-clause attribution misframing
holes_addressed.description: The post-EXP-224 double-bind paragraph framed the Fail-clause as a dome 'concession' that the dome 'does not disambiguate.' The Fail-clause is in fact our steel-man codification (explicitly labeled 'our Pass band' in the preceding paragraph), so the sentences read as if we manufactured a Fail-clause the dome never published and then faulted the dome for not resolving our own ambiguity. A dome defender's single strongest counter-move on the current section.
holes_addressed.fix: Patch A owns the codification explicitly ('Our codified Fail-clause ... that we appended to our Pass band') and reroutes the disambiguation burden to 'the dome has not published any falsification criterion specific enough to disambiguate' — a critique of missing-specification rather than dome-authored-ambiguity. Patch B replaces 'The dome does not publish which reading governs' with 'The dome has not committed to either disambiguation.' The forced-disjunction conclusion is untouched.

new_evidence_added: Parenthetical reference to PRED-095 as registered in data/predictions.json containing no Chapman-language Fail criterion — already used upstream in the same paragraph's threshold-codification sub-paragraph; no new citation.

anticipated_objections.objection: The new opening sentence is ~30 words longer. Does it weaken readability?
anticipated_objections.response: The original was 28 words and carried a rhetorical vulnerability (attribution misframing) the curmudgeon flagged at moderate severity. The replacement is 55 words and carries a parenthetical that explicitly names the missing-specification critique. The net is ~27 extra words in exchange for closing the defender's strongest attack on this section. c5 Hole 3 minor flags the whole paragraph as long — a future polish EXP could split the paragraph; this EXP keeps scope to the attribution fix.

anticipated_objections.objection: Patch B only changes 'publish which reading governs' to 'committed to either disambiguation.' Is the semantic shift load-bearing?
anticipated_objections.response: Yes: 'publish which reading governs' presupposes there is a dome-authored reading to govern; 'committed to either disambiguation' is neutral on whether the dome is the original author. The shift aligns with Patch A's ownership of the codification and preserves the double-bind conclusion (interpretation A surrenders discrimination; interpretation B makes Pass and Fail incompatible).

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not also take c5 Hole 2 ('mutually exclusive' vs 'overlap' phrasing) and Hole 3 (paragraph-split readability) in the same patch?
anticipated_objections.response:
Hole 2 (minor) is a precision tweak in the interpretation-B clause; it is a separate substring with its own anchor and can be a follow-up micro-patch. Hole 3 (minor) is a structural split of a ~600-word paragraph — substantial enough to warrant its own EXP with its own HTML

boundary edits. Bundling either into this EXP would widen the anchor footprint. The attribution fix (moderate) is the operative defender-weakening; it should land alone.

anticipated_objections.objection: PRED-095 already appears in the threshold-codification paragraph upstream — doesn't adding another PRED-095 reference in the new parenthetical double up?
anticipated_objections.response: The threshold-codification paragraph cites PRED-095 positively ('dome registration 2026-03-12 via Git + OpenTimestamps'). The new parenthetical cites it in the narrower role of 'the registered claim contains no Chapman-language Fail criterion' — a different operative fact. Both references are load-bearing for different arguments; the repetition is informative, not redundant.

kernel_of_truth: The curmudgeon is right that a fair-audit double-bind only has force if the reader accepts the Fail-clause is a fair criterion to impose. The old text took a shortcut by framing the clause as dome-authored, which let us put the disambiguation burden on the dome without having to defend the imposition. Owning the codification openly is more work — we now have to justify why the Pass band needs any Fail sentence at all — but it is the honest framing, and c5's stronger-arguments[1] suggests it actually strengthens the double-bind by reframing it as 'the burden to specify a non-ambiguous falsifier is on the prediction author, not the auditor.' The new parenthetical carries that burden-shift explicitly.
cross_win_consistency_flag: The attribution pattern (codification-owned vs framed-as-dome-authored) should be checked across other steel-man passages where we impose fair-audit falsifiers (Sections 8b.1 sequenced-read Pass band and 8b.2 PRED-069 Tier 3). If similar attribution misframings exist, flag as additional issues; this EXP only touches 8b.3.
out_of_scope_deliberately: c5 Hole 2 (minor) — 'mutually exclusive' vs 'overlap' phrasing in interpretation-B clause. Follow-up micro-patch.
c5 Hole 3 (minor) — split the ~600-word L3a paragraph at the double-bind boundary. Separate structural EXP.

Pre-existing c4 Holes 2/3/4/6/7 — globe-parasitism overreach, inject_ai_layer.py family relationship, Tier-3 label conflation, station-cluster boundary, 'prior eclipse' vagueness. All flagged in c5 deeper_analysis for future follow-up; none in scope for ISS-1422.
No changes to SEC-8b.1, SEC-8b.2, SEC-8b.4, or the threshold-codification sub-paragraph of SEC-8b.3.
propagation_note: EXP-224 integrated the Fail-clause forced-disjunction double-bind from ISS-1342. c5 review confirmed the structure is sound but flagged the attribution framing as moderate. EXP-230 closes that last defender-accessible hole on the section without reopening the EXP-224 structural work.
